title: Model empirical undercut/overcut threat, not just tyre age
summary: Official F1 strategy analysis emphasizes that undercut strength depends on degradation, warm-up penalty, and being within striking range, while recent AutoF1 work explicitly added surrounding-driver distance features for undercut/overcut decisions.
rationale: Official F1 strategy analysis emphasizes that undercut strength depends on degradation, warm-up penalty, and being within striking range, while recent AutoF1 work explicitly added surrounding-driver distance features for undercut/overcut decisions. Your current best features include some same-lap tyre context, but not an explicit race-specific estimate of whether pitting now is advantaged or punished.
implementation_hint: Create race-level or race-compound priors for fresh-tyre out-lap tax and expected undercut payoff from training data, then combine them with same-lap position-neighbor tyre age and pace deltas. Useful features: inferred undercut threat, overcut viability, and pit-benefit minus expected warm-up loss.
expected_effect: Should improve ranking for borderline cases where tyre wear alone is insufficient and strategy is driven by track-specific out-lap behavior.
risk: Without actual gap-to-car-ahead/behind, these are proxies and may be noisy or race-specific.
